channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Afghan women’s group hails court's move to arrest Taliban leaders for persecution of women
item.description: An Afghan women’s group on Friday hailed a decision by the International Criminal Court to arrest Taliban leaders for their persecution of women.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers to cast accuser as money seeker in opening statements at trial
item.description: A$AP Rocky is the celebrity in the room, but A$AP Relli will star at his trial. The hip-hop star, fashion maven and actor, whose legal name is Rakim Athelaston Mayers, is accused of firing a gun at his former friend Relli, born Terell Ephron, on whose testimony prosecutors are pinning their case.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Carolina drunk driver arrested in December had been deported 3 times: affidavit
item.description: An illegal immigrant arrested for drunk driving in South Carolina has a lengthy criminal history in the United States and had been deported three times in less than five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rhode Island judge specializing in immigration law resigns after FBI raids office
item.description: The FBI and Providence, Rhode Island, police raided a Central Falls Municipal Court judge's office Thursday, though the reasons are unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Our solar system is about to put on a show
item.description: Keep your eyes on the sky. Six planets in our solar system are coming into alignment and will be visible from Earth. AccuWeather says Saturn, Venus, Neptune, Uranus, Jupiter and Mars will be visible every evening through the first half of February, appearing in a line across the sky.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KTLA 5

item.title: Colorado DNA analyst appears on forgery charges as validity of more than 500 cases in doubt
item.description: GOLDEN, Colo. (AP) — A former Colorado Bureau of Investigation DNA scientist is facing 102 criminal charges, including dozens of counts of forgery, after authorities said she cut corners and didn't follow standard testing protocols in hundreds of criminal cases.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge, John C. Coughenour, temporarily blocked President Donald Trump's executive order attempting to end birthright citizenship, citing it as blatantly unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Jason Nguyen, Robert Arnold, KPRC 2

item.title: California will spend $2.5 billion to help the Los Angeles area recover from wildfires
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has signed a more than $2.5 billion package to help the Los Angeles area recover from its recent deadly wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration blocks federal health agencies from communicating with public
item.description: The Trump administration has put a freeze on many federal health agency communications with the public through at least the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE and JONEL ALECCIA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats weighing limits on presidential pardons after Trump, Biden moves
item.description: House Democrats are calling for Congress to rein in the president’s pardon powers after President Trump’s mass clemency for the Jan. 6 mob and President Biden’s reprieve for certain family members.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump lists 'number of reasons' for pardoning violent Jan. 6 rioters
item.description: President Donald Trump listed a “number of reasons” for pardoning violent Jan. 6 rioters during an interview aired Wednesday night with Fox News’s Sean Hannity.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lawsuit claims Indiana Catholic school turned blind eye to child porn scheme; failed to protect students
item.description: And the lawsuit also claims that Bishop Luers officials not only knew all about the scheme - supposedly perpetrated by at least three boys and involving dozens of girls - but failed to report it to authorities while also trying to stonewall giving any updates of a supposed investigation to the girls
item.dc:creator: Jeff Wiehe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gulf of Whatnow? Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: Mapmakers and teachers are re-thinking what to call the gulf of water between Mexico, the United States and Cuba after President Donald Trump ordered it renamed from the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Laurie Kellman And Hillel Italie, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nancy Leftenant-Colon, who was the first Black woman in Army Nurse Corps, has died
item.description: The first Black woman to join the U.S. Army Nurse Corps after the military was desegregated in the 1940s has died. She was 104.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Communities walk tightrope as Trump ramps up immigration enforcement, threatens tariffs
item.description: The mayor El Paso and his chief of police on Wednesday said they will honor calls from federal law enforcement partners in need of assistance but will not volunteer municipal officers for immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Nearly half of remote workers say they'd quit before returning to office full-time
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Nearly half of workers who currently work remotely at least some of the time would likely leave their jobs if ordered to return to the office full-time. At least, that's the key finding in a new survey from the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: Meta is pushing back on claims that Facebook and Instagram users are being forced into following accounts for President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Trump’s blanket Jan. 6 pardons stun Republicans on Capitol Hill
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping pardons of more than 1,500 people charged with crimes related to the Capitol riot of Jan. 6, 2021, including individuals who assaulted police officers, stunned Republican lawmakers who witnessed firsthand the chaos on Capitol Hill four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Mittens the cat becomes an accidental frequent flyer after getting mistakenly left on a plane
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: China and US partners are moving closer as Trump returns to the White House
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's relations are starting to improve with Japan, India and other countries that former U.S. President Joe Biden courted, just as Donald Trump brings his more unilateralist approach back to the White House.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Colorado woman ID'ed as driver in crash that killed officer
item.description: The driver of a vehicle that collided head on with a Navasota police vehicle killing Sgt. Mark Butler has been identified as 59-year-old Kimberly Lannigan, of Colorado Springs, Colorado. She was also killed in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, Fox 5

item.title: US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont near Canada
item.description: Authorities on Tuesday were investigating a fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent that also left a suspect dead and another injured on a Vermont highway near Canada, authorities said. Agent David Maland was killed Monday afternoon following a traffic stop, a U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 11-year-old pulls ultra rare Paul Skenes rookie card, Pirates says massive trade deal stands
item.description: The card, which contains the "Debut Patch" worn by Paul Skenes, quickly became a highly-coveted item. After months of search, an 11-year-old has reportedly come up with it.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: Nose spray to treat depression? FDA approves first-of-its-kind medicine
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved a nose spray to help treat depression, Johnson & Johnson announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WGN 9

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, ABC 4
